X-RateLimit-Remaining.description: The number of requests left for the time window.

schema.description: The number of requests left for the time window.

X-RateLimit-Reset.description: The UTC date/time at which the current rate limit window resets.

schema.description: The UTC date/time at which the current rate limit window resets.

delete_device_function.description: Device function for which the local object needs to be deleted. Mandatory if object_type = LOCAL. Should not be provided for object_type = SHARED.
delete_device_function.name: device-function

delete_object_type.description: LOCAL - To delete local objects. SHARED - To delete shared objects. Default - Defaults to SHARED if not provided
delete_object_type.name: object-type

delete_scope_id.description: Scope at which local object needs to be deleted. Mandatory if object-type = LOCAL. Should not be provided for object-type = SHARED.
delete_scope_id.name: scope-id

detailed.description: True - Includes annotations in the returned json to indicate the type of object, scope, and device function. False - Returns only config objects without annotations.
detailed.name: detailed

effective.description: True - Returns effective configuration (i.e hierarchically merged configuration). False - Returns committed configuration at a given scope.
effective.name: effective

get_device_function.description: Filters configuration specific to given device-function type. If not provided and view_type = LOCAL, then defaults to all device-function's.
get_device_function.name: device-function

get_object_type.description: Retrieves LOCAL or SHARED or both(default) configuration objects.
get_object_type.name: object-type

get_scope_id.description: Configuration at a given scope. Mandatory for view-type = LOCAL.
get_scope_id.name: scope-id

patch_device_function.description: Device function for which the local object needs to be updated. Mandatory if object_type = LOCAL. Should not be provided for object_type = SHARED.
patch_device_function.name: device-function

patch_object_type.description: LOCAL - To update local objects. SHARED - To update shared objects. Default - Defaults to SHARED if not provided
patch_object_type.name: object-type

patch_scope_id.description: Scope at which local object needs to be updated. Mandatory if object-type = LOCAL. Should not be provided for object-type = SHARED.
patch_scope_id.name: scope-id

post_device_function.description: Device function for which the local object needs to be created. Mandatory if object_type = LOCAL. Should not be provided for object_type = SHARED.
post_device_function.name: device-function

post_object_type.description: LOCAL - To create local objects. SHARED - To create shared objects. Default - Defaults to SHARED if not provided
post_object_type.name: object-type

post_scope_id.description: Scope at which local object needs to be created. Mandatory if object-type = LOCAL. Should not be provided for object-type = SHARED.
post_scope_id.name: scope-id

view_type.description: LIBRARY - Returns shared objects in library. For LIBRARY, all other parameters listed below, except 'detailed' , will be ignored. LOCAL - Returns configurations for a scope and one or more device function based on parameters below.
view_type.name: view-type

ArubaAppBandwidthContract_AppBandwidthCo.description:
App Bandwidth Contracts. This container holds named profiles that define
directional (UPSTREAM/DOWNSTREAM) bandwidth limits for application traffic.
Each profile can include four different contract types: DPI application based
contracts (app), DPI application category based contracts (app-category), WebCC
reputation score/risk based contracts (web-reputation), and WebCC category based
contracts (web-category). Each contract entry uses a composite key leaf that
combines the identifier and direction. Bandwidth limits are configured by
selecting bandwidth units (kbits/s or mbits/s) and specifying a bit-rate; the
bit-rate is validated by a YANG must constraint with unit-specific ranges
(256..2000000 for KBITS_PER_SEC and 1..2000 for MBITS_PER_SEC).
The combined total number of entries across all four contract lists (app,
app-category, web-reputation, and web-category) within a single profile
must not exceed 32.

profiles.description:
List of App Bandwidth Contract profiles. Each profile is identified by a
unique name and groups up to four types of directional bandwidth contracts:
DPI application (app), DPI application category (app-category), WebCC
reputation score (web-reputation), and WebCC category (web-category).
The combined total number of entries across all four contract lists within
a single profile must not exceed 32.

app.description:
List of bandwidth contracts keyed by DPI application name and traffic direction.
Each entry maps a specific application (e.g. facebook, youtube, zoom) to a
bandwidth contract for either upstream or downstream traffic. The application
name must be a valid DPI application type and the direction must be specified.
Bandwidth limits are configured via the bwc-units and bit-rate leaves.

app-name-direction.description: Key combination of DPI Application and direction separated by '~'.

app-category.description:
List of bandwidth contracts keyed by DPI application category and traffic direction.
Each entry maps a broad application category (e.g. STREAMING, GAMING,
SOCIAL-NETWORKING) to a bandwidth contract for either upstream or downstream
traffic. This allows bandwidth policies to be applied at the category level
rather than per individual application. Bandwidth limits are configured via
the bwc-units and bit-rate leaves.

app-category-name-direction.description: Key combination of DPI Application Category and direction separated by '~'.

web-category.description:
List of bandwidth contracts keyed by WebCC category and traffic direction.
WebCC classifies web destinations into categories (e.g. BUSINESS-ECONOMY,
GAMES, EDUCATIONAL-INSTITUTIONS, SOCIAL-NETWORKING). Each entry maps one
WebCC category to a bandwidth contract for either upstream or downstream
traffic, enabling bandwidth policies based on the type of web content being
accessed. There can be at most one contract per category per direction.

web-category-name-direction.description: Key combination of WebCC category and direction separated by '~'.

web-reputation.description:
List of bandwidth contracts keyed by WebCC reputation score range and traffic
direction. WebCC assigns a reputation score to each URL upon classification and
groups scores into the following risk levels: TRUSTWORTHY (81-100),
LOW_RISK (61-80), MODERATE_RISK (41-60), SUSPICIOUS (21-40), and
HIGH_RISK (1-20). Each entry maps one risk level to a bandwidth contract for
either upstream or downstream traffic, allowing differentiated bandwidth
treatment based on the trustworthiness of web destinations. There can be at
most one contract per risk level per direction.

web-rep-score-direction.description: Key combination of WebCC reputation score and direction separated by '~'.

app.description:
List of bandwidth contracts keyed by DPI application name and traffic direction.
Each entry maps a specific application (e.g. facebook, youtube, zoom) to a
bandwidth contract for either upstream or downstream traffic. The application
name must be a valid DPI application type and the direction must be specified.
Bandwidth limits are configured via the bwc-units and bit-rate leaves.

app-name-direction.description: Key combination of DPI Application and direction separated by '~'.

app-category.description:

Each entry maps a broad application category (e.g. STREAMING, GAMING,

rather than per individual application. Bandwidth limits are configured via

app-category-name-direction.description: Key combination of DPI Application Category and direction separated by '~'.

ArubaAppBandwidthContract_BwcRate.description: Bandwidth contract upstream/downstream rate in kbps or mbps.

bit-rate.description: Bandwidth in kbits per second or mbits per second.

bwc-units.description:
Select method to specify bandwidth units - either kbits or mbits per second.
This configuration is mandatory.

ArubaAppBandwidthContract_BwcRateGet.description: Bandwidth contract upstream/downstream rate in kbps or mbps.

bit-rate.description: Bandwidth in kbits per second or mbits per second.

bwc-units.description:
Select method to specify bandwidth units - either kbits or mbits per second.
This configuration is mandatory.

ArubaAppRecogControl_ArcSchemaGET.description:
Configuration of Application Recognition & Control (ARC) global profile(s).
These APIs are applicable to AP, Gateway & CX-Switch devices only.

cache-state.description: Enable DPI classification cache. Applicable to AP only.

description.description: Configure a profile description. Only alphanumeric characters, space, hyphen, underscore, hash, and period are allowed.

firewall-visibility.description: Enable Firewall visibility feature for monitoring. Applicable to Gateways only.

limit-exceed-action.description: Apply Application Based Policy, session limit exceed action. Applicable to CX-Switch only.

x-enumDescriptions.DROP_NEW_FLOWS:
Drop all new flows associated with clients that have an Application
Based Policy configured when the session table is full.
x-enumDescriptions.LOG_ONLY:
Log new flows for which session table entries could not be created
and forward traffic without Application Based Policy inspection.

limit-exceed-action.x-typeDescription: Application Based Policy, session limit exceed action types.

name.description: Configure a profile name. Only alphanumeric characters, space, hyphen, underscore, hash, and period are allowed.

recog-types.description: App recognition types and parameter configurations.

app-perf-monitoring.description: Enabling Application performance monitoring. This option is applicable only APs, GWs

dpi-enable.description:
Enable App Recognition features. This field enables Deep Packet Inspection (DPI) for AP, Gateway and CX Switches.
It also implicitly enables Web Content Classification (WebCC) for AP and Gateway only.
This is enabled by default for Microbranch & Campus APs, Mobility and Branch Gateways.
DPI uses Qosmos for classifying traffic into application & application category types.

webcc-cache-miss-drop.description:
WebCC caches classification data of a URL when it encounters it for the first time.
Enable this feature to drop session packets until a cache entry is found for the URL, i.e. until a WebCC
category or reputation score classification is available in the internal WebCC cache.
This feature is applicable to Gateway only. It is disabled by default.

webcc-enable.description:
Enable Web Content Classification (WebCC) feature for AP and Gateway.
This is enabled by default for Microbranch & Campus APs, Mobility and Branch Gateways.
It is mandatory to enable DPI to use WebCC.  WebCC feature uses the Brightcloud® URL database of categorie and
reputation risk scores to classify web traffic. An IP name server must be configured for this feature to work.

redirect-url.description:
Configure URL to redirect session to when users access a URL that matches a WebCC deny rule.
The URL must be prefixed with http:// or https:// . Permitted length is 5-250 characters.
Applicable to Gateway only.
redirect-url.x-typeDescription:
An ASCII-encoded Uniform Resource Locator (URL) as defined
in RFC 3986, section 1.1.3

url-visibility.description: Enable the URL visibility feature. Applicable to AP only.

webcc-connectiontype-ipv6.description:
Enable to force WebCC to use IPv6 for communicating with vendor service.
This configuration is disabled by default.
Disabling it makes WebCC fall back to dual-stack mode with IPv4 preferred.
This feature is applicable to Gateways only.

cache-state.description: Enable DPI classification cache. Applicable to AP only.

description.description: Configure a profile description. Only alphanumeric characters, space, hyphen, underscore, hash, and period are allowed.

firewall-visibility.description: Enable Firewall visibility feature for monitoring. Applicable to Gateways only.

limit-exceed-action.description: Apply Application Based Policy, session limit exceed action. Applicable to CX-Switch only.

x-enumDescriptions.DROP_NEW_FLOWS:
Drop all new flows associated with clients that have an Application
Based Policy configured when the session table is full.
x-enumDescriptions.LOG_ONLY:
Log new flows for which session table entries could not be created
and forward traffic without Application Based Policy inspection.

limit-exceed-action.x-typeDescription: Application Based Policy, session limit exceed action types.

name.description: Configure a profile name. Only alphanumeric characters, space, hyphen, underscore, hash, and period are allowed.

recog-types.description: App recognition types and parameter configurations.

app-perf-monitoring.description: Enabling Application performance monitoring. This option is applicable only APs, GWs

ArubaDapApplication_DapApplicationSchema.description:
Configure a DAP application profile is for optimizing this application. One DAP SLA profile
has to be configured under the DAP application profile knob to indicate how to optimize this
application. The application profile number can be up to 32.

sla-profile.description: The DAP SLA profile which can be referenced by current DAP application profile.

carrier-name.description: Carrier name of the fqdn list.

dns-pattern.description: WiFi calling DNS patterns of the ePDG (Evolved Packet Data Gateway)

items.description: WiFi calling DNS patterns of the ePDG (Evolved Packet Data Gateway)

sla-profile.description: The DAP SLA profile which can be referenced by current DAP application profile.

carrier-name.description: Carrier name of the fqdn list.

dns-pattern.description: WiFi calling DNS patterns of the ePDG (Evolved Packet Data Gateway)

items.description: WiFi calling DNS patterns of the ePDG (Evolved Packet Data Gateway)

sla-profile.description: The DAP SLA profile which can be referenced by current DAP application profile.

carrier-name.description: Carrier name of the fqdn list.

dns-pattern.description: WiFi calling DNS patterns of the ePDG (Evolved Packet Data Gateway)

items.description: WiFi calling DNS patterns of the ePDG (Evolved Packet Data Gateway)

ArubaDapSla_DapSlaSchemaGET.description:
Dynamic application prioritization(DAP) SLA profile allow to configure some SLA parameters such as latency,
jitter, and so on, based on which some specific applications can be optimized. A name is a unique identifier
that names an DAP SLA profile, the SLA profile numbers can be up to 32.

jitter.description: Average Jitter - variation in the time it takes for data packets to arrive at a destination over a network.

latency.description: One way delay - Time it takes for data to pass from one point on a network to another.

max-si.description: Max Service Interval - minimum interval between the start of two consecutive Service Periods.

min-si.description: Min Service Interval - minimum interval between the start of two consecutive Service periods.

msdu-lifetime.description: Max Delay bound - maximum amount of time, in milliseconds, since the arrival of the MSDU at the MAC data service interface beyond which the MSDU is not useful even if received by the receiver.

msdu-size.description: Max packet size -  maximum size of an MSDU belonging to the traffic flow described by this element.

jitter.description: Average Jitter - variation in the time it takes for data packets to arrive at a destination over a network.

latency.description: One way delay - Time it takes for data to pass from one point on a network to another.

max-si.description: Max Service Interval - minimum interval between the start of two consecutive Service Periods.

min-si.description: Min Service Interval - minimum interval between the start of two consecutive Service periods.

msdu-lifetime.description: Max Delay bound - maximum amount of time, in milliseconds, since the arrival of the MSDU at the MAC data service interface beyond which the MSDU is not useful even if received by the receiver.

msdu-size.description: Max packet size -  maximum size of an MSDU belonging to the traffic flow described by this element.

msdu-lifetime.description: Max Delay bound - maximum amount of time, in milliseconds, since the arrival of the MSDU at the MAC data service interface beyond which the MSDU is not useful even if received by the receiver.

msdu-size.description: Max packet size -  maximum size of an MSDU belonging to the traffic flow described by this element.

ArubaDap_DapSchemaGET.description:
Dynamic application prioritization(DAP) is an application-aware QoS feature.
Unlike traditional QoS methods that treat all traffic matching a classification the same,
dynamic application prioritization adjusts based on each individual application’s needs.
Dynamic Application Priorization automatically adjusts the advanced RF parameters such as
uplink-scheduling to satisfy stringent SLAs of business-critical applications. This ensures
improved user experience even in the most challenging RF conditions.

profile.description: Dynamic application prioritization(DAP) global profile. Each AP only support one profile.

dyn-app-prioritization.description:
Enable DAP policy. This is a global DAP switch, must configure both of
this switch and per-role DAP switch to make DAP policy work.

port.description: Custom SIP port for parsing SIP control packets
port.x-typeDescription:
A 16-bit port number used by a transport protocol such as TCP
or UDP.

protocol.description: TCP or UDP are the only two options supported for SIP protocol

timeout-min.description: Timeout for the UDP or TCP SIP protocol control plane sessions in minutes

voice-tracking-snmptrap.description: Enables sending SNMP Traps to SNMP server when voice calls are detected

dyn-app-prioritization.description:
Enable DAP policy. This is a global DAP switch, must configure both of
this switch and per-role DAP switch to make DAP policy work.

port.description: Custom SIP port for parsing SIP control packets
port.x-typeDescription:
A 16-bit port number used by a transport protocol such as TCP
or UDP.

protocol.description: TCP or UDP are the only two options supported for SIP protocol

timeout-min.description: Timeout for the UDP or TCP SIP protocol control plane sessions in minutes

voice-tracking-snmptrap.description: Enables sending SNMP Traps to SNMP server when voice calls are detected

voice-tracking-snmptrap.description: Enables sending SNMP Traps to SNMP server when voice calls are detected

get.description:
Configuration of Application Recognition & Control (ARC) global profile(s).
These APIs are applicable to AP, Gateway & CX-Switch devices only.

get.description:
Dynamic application prioritization(DAP) is an application-aware QoS feature.
Unlike traditional QoS methods that treat all traffic matching a classification the same,
dynamic application prioritization adjusts based on each individual application’s needs.
Dynamic Application Priorization automatically adjusts the advanced RF parameters such as
uplink-scheduling to satisfy stringent SLAs of business-critical applications. This ensures
improved user experience even in the most challenging RF conditions.

Configure a DAP application profile is for optimizing this application. One DAP SLA profile
has to be configured under the DAP application profile knob to indicate how to optimize this
application. The application profile number can be up to 32.

get.summary: Read existing DAP application profile configuration

patch.summary: Update existing DAP application profile configuration

post.summary: Create a new DAP application profile by unique name

put.summary: Create or Update existing DAP application profile configuration

get.description:
Dynamic application prioritization(DAP) SLA profile allow to configure some SLA parameters such as latency,
jitter, and so on, based on which some specific applications can be optimized. A name is a unique identifier
that names an DAP SLA profile, the SLA profile numbers can be up to 32.

get.summary: Read existing DAP SLA profile configuration

patch.summary: Update existing DAP SLA profile configuration

post.summary: Create a new DAP SLA profile by unique name

put.summary: Create or Update existing DAP SLA profile configuration
